item.title: Trump Rejects Need for Curbs on Oil Exports After Trade Surge
item.description: President Donald Trump said there’s no need for curbs on US oil or jet fuel exports to address global shortages sparked by the war with Iran.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Says He Would Join Trump’s China Trip If Invited
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang said he would gladly join President Donald Trump on an upcoming visit to China if invited, signaling that the leader of America’s most valuable company has yet to receive an offer to go.

item.title: Australia Moves to Toughen Pension Funds’ Performance Scorecard
item.description: The Australian government is moving to tighten an annual performance test for the nation’s A$4.5 trillion ($3.2 trillion) pension industry as concerns grow the current model may be steering investment away from some sectors.

item.title: Australia to ‘Try to Thread the Needle’ in Budget Tested by War
item.description: Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers aims to deliver a budget that tackles inflation, revives productivity and reins in deficits, a tough challenge at a time when fuel prices are soaring because of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Singapore Tests Two for Hantavirus After Deadly Cruise Outbreak
item.description: Singapore has isolated two residents who were onboard a cruise ship linked to a deadly outbreak of hantavirus.

item.title: Japan’s Real Wages Rise For Third Month to Keep BOJ on Hike Path
item.description: Japanese workers’ real wages rose in March for a third consecutive month, supporting the Bank of Japan’s case for further interest rate hikes even as the Middle East conflict clouds the economic outlook.

item.title: Foreign Central Banks Boost Share of Malaysian Bonds to Record
item.description: Foreign central banks and governments have raised their share of Malaysian sovereign bonds to a record, underscoring the nation’s growing appeal as a reserve asset in a volatile global environment.

item.title: Asia Heat Waves Spell Double Trouble for Economies Hit by Oil
item.description: Asia is grappling with a new threat to inflation on top of an oil shock as a looming El Niño brings soaring temperatures and dry weather to countries from India to New Zealand, driving up food costs.

item.title: MP Sees Heavy Rare Earth Demand Falling as Technology Advances
item.description: MP Materials Corp. expects demand for some of the most expensive rare earth materials to drop sharply as magnet makers adopt alternative metals.

item.title: Columbia, Stanford Report Online Outages Amid Cyber Incident
item.description: Universities across the US reported widespread outages on Thursday after a cybersecurity incident affected the Canvas online learning platform used by colleges nationwide.

item.title: Gold Steadies as US-Iran Clashes Dim Truce Prospects in Mideast
item.description: Gold was little changed as optimism that a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz was imminent faded amid reports of attacks on US Navy vessels, reviving inflation concerns.

item.title: Google Judge Says Too Early to Pause DOJ Remedy in Search Case
item.description: A federal judge rejected Alphabet Inc.’s request to pause an order that forces the tech giant to provide rivals with access to its underlying search data while the company appeals a ruling that it illegally monopolized the online search market.

item.title: Macquarie Profit Jumps, Commodities Chief Paid $25.5 Million
item.description: Macquarie Group Ltd.’s commodities and global markets division chief Simon Wright got a pay bump after profit within the bank’s key business drove earnings growth at the Australian firm.

item.title: Texas Roadhouse Sales Hold as Diners Pick Cheaper Beef Cuts
item.description: Texas Roadhouse Inc. shares climbed after the restaurant operator posted in-line first‑quarter results and signaled that sales momentum would continue as diners adjusted to higher prices and commodity-cost inflation moderates slightly.

item.title: US Stock Futures Fall, Oil Climbs on Iran Tensions: Markets Wrap
item.description: US stock futures fell in early Friday trading and oil rose as escalating conflict in the Middle East rattled investors and revived concerns about energy supplies.

item.title: Compass Breaks 5-Year Brazil IPO Drought With $650 Million Offer
item.description: Compass Gas e Energia SA’s shareholders raised 3.2 billion reais ($650 million) the first initial public offering in Brazil in almost five years, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Oil Jumps Following Fresh Clashes Between US and Iranian Forces
item.description: Oil jumped following renewed clashes between US and Iranian forces, jeopardizing the outlook for a deal to end the 10-week war.

item.title: SoftBank Rally Hinges on OpenAI Growth Easing Balance Sheet Fear
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp.’s stock rally faces a test against reality next week, with investors clamoring for assurance that the company’s multibillion-dollar bet on an increasingly embattled OpenAI will be worth the money.

item.title: $10 Billion Golub Fund Caps Outflows After Requests for 8.5%
item.description: Golub Capital is capping withdrawals from its private credit fund after investors sought to pull 8.5% of shares, the latest instance of a money manager restricting outflows amid a wave of redemption requests.

item.title: Blackstone-Led Group Set to Inject $100 Million Into Medallia
item.description: A group of private credit investors led by Blackstone Inc. is preparing to inject at least $100 million of fresh funds into Medallia Inc. as part of a restructuring that will put them in control of the software maker, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Wynn Expects ‘Modest Delay’ in Opening of New Middle East Casino
item.description: Wynn Resorts Ltd. Chief Executive Officer Craig Billings said the company now expects a “modest delay” in the opening date of a new casino resort it’s building in the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Cloudflare Forecast Misses Estimates, Announces Job Cuts
item.description: Cloudflare Inc. stock fell on Thursday after the company said it would slash jobs and gave a forecast for revenue that fell short of analysts’ expectations.

item.title: The AI Revival of the Three Mile Island Nuclear Plant
item.description: Microsoft’s vast power demands are bringing together two transformative—and risky—technologies.

item.title: World’s Most Important Bromance Revived as Trump Heads to China
item.description: When Donald Trump’s plane touches down in Beijing, he’ll become the first sitting US president to visit China in nearly a decade. His two-day summit with Xi Jinping extends a personal relationship that’s weathered a tariff war, pandemic and historic energy crisis.

item.title: Cloudflare to Cut One-Fifth of Workers in Move to AI-First Model
item.description: Cloudflare Inc. plans to cut more than 1,100 jobs globally as it accelerates its shift to an agentic AI-first operating model.

item.title: DraftKings Profit Beats Estimates as Sportsbook Sales Grow
item.description: DraftKings Inc. reported revenue that rose 17% to $1.65 billion, slightly ahead of analysts’ estimates for $1.63 billion.

item.title: Nvidia to Invest Up to $2.1 Billion in Data Center Firm IREN
item.description: Nvidia Corp. is investing as much as $2.1 billion in the data center developer IREN Ltd. as part of a broader partnership between the two companies aimed at accelerating the construction of artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: The Fed’s Susan Collins on FOMC Dissent and the Warsh Transition
item.description: On today’s Big Take podcast, Boston Fed President Susan Collins on recent FOMC dissent, Kevin Warsh’s nomination and where interest rates could land in 2026.

item.title: Elliott Said to Tap Banks to Run Barnes & Noble’s London IPO
item.description: Elliott Management Corp. has selected banks to lead a combined initial public offering of book retailers Barnes & Noble Inc. and Waterstones Booksellers Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Coinbase Swings to a Loss While Posting Another Revenue Drop
item.description: Just days after announcing deep job cuts made to manage costs in volatile markets and to harness advances in artificial intelligence, Coinbase Global Inc. showed how substantially a lingering crypto bear market can weigh on results.
